turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: We'll hear argument now in number oh oh five sixty-eight, New York versus Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and the companion case, oh oh eight oh nine Mr. Malone.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court. From nineteen ten until the issuance of the FERC decision under review, all fifty States regulated transmissions of electricity to retail customers. You didn't see the rates, terms, and conditions of that regulation, with a few exceptions, because the rates were bundled in bills that charged customers for all of the elements of retail service which are basically the energy itself, or the commodity, its delivery over transmission high voltage and distribution low voltage lines, and metering and billing. The issue in this case is whether the nineteen thirty-five Congress intended the Federal Power Commission, now FERC, to displace the State laws that required that regulation from nineteen ten until nineteen ninety-six if the commodity, the energy not the transmission, but the commodity is unbundled from the retail customers' bills. The FERC, while conceding that unbundling was never contemplated by the nineteen thirty-five Congress, nonetheless has taken the position that if the commodity comes out of the bill, in order to give the customer knowledge to effect a choice to buy energy from a utility or a non-utility, then the transmission, although it still remains bundled with other elements and is never shown on the bill, except in the State of California, becomes a new and separate service and that that transmission is subject to preemption so that the States have to leave the scene...

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: for only FERC... What if there, what if there...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: were no statute at all? And , do you think that under the Commerce Clause, with today's multi-state grids for transmission of power, that the States could have directly regulated energy transmission as a separate service?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: I don't think there's any question, Your Honor, that under today's Commerce Clause jurisprudence, the States would be able to regulate retail deliveries over transmission facilities, particularly within their own States.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: But the statute does give the FERC jurisdiction over the transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Exactly.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: attempting to fill...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: we didn't have these interstate grids, and isn't it conceivable that you could have a power company producing power within a State and distributing it to customers within the State, everything totally intrastate? That may have been a common occurrence at the time the statute was passed.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Well, Your Honor, the rate of interaconnection[:interconnection] from nineteen twenty to nineteen thirty-five was actually greater than the rate of interconnection that we have seen since.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Now that, that...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: are you saying o- one o- either of these two things? Either transmission regardless of the rate at which it is billed is is not essentially interstate commerce, or are you saying that you simply cannot read the act to pick up that activity in interstate commerce?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: I'm saying that the l- the latter with with one caveat.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: So, you, w- do you admit then that th- that it is interstate commerce.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: It clearly is subject to regulation by Congress if it wants to.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: We don't need th- the States do not need to get into the question of which transmissions are in interstate commerce and which aren't in order to preserve its jurisdiction over retail transmissions because Congress wanted the States to be able to continue to regulate retail transmissions.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: w- w- This is what I don't understand.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Why isn't...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: why isn't that the way to look at it rather than...

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Be- Because the th- a- as the Colton case held, there's a there's a bright line here between retail and wholesale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: said.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: All right.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: But you you seem to concede and I don't see why you do it that the right to regulate transmission includes the right to regulate sale.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: No, we we we don't.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: We don't concede that.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: What what I'm saying is that when unbundles when one unbundles transmission, the States don't lose it, even if it's out there by itself, if it's part of retail.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: L- Let Let me give an example.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: depend on what sale means? If it's sale of the commodity, it says, sale of such energy at wholesale in interstate commerce.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Because when le- le- let's go back to nineteen thirty-five, Your Honor.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: bill, you mean the bill that the customer gets.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Now now, now this Court has said many time, Your Honor, your, times, Your Honor I know, Justice Scalia, you don't like reference to legislative history, but you do accept ref- reading provisions in harmony.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Those prov-

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Where do we find these?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: They ar- they are I'm sorry, Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: And whereabouts? Is i- do we Starting with six fifty-two A?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: The I d- I don't have the the the page reference off the top of my head.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Well, you're you're summarizing a st- a series of statutory provisions in those words?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: over...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: give us the USC cites of those things? I, It's crazy when you people who spend your whole life in this one statute use the old the old numbers instead of the United States Code numbers.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You're talking about sixteen USC eight twenty-four A and B. Right?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: That's correct.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: You're talking now about section A?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Well, just on that, is it the case that if in nineteen thirty-five Duke Power owns generating facilities in West Virginia, owns lines from West Virginia to North Carolina, and owns a distribution company in North Carolina, all one company, does the FPC have any jurisdiction at all in your opinion?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: If there's a sale for resale...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: No, no no.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: No.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: you're saying.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: The the, there is a case in nineteen thirty-four at two PUR second, where it's it's almost your iden- your hypothetical.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: All right.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: That's correct.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: That's your argument.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Mist-, Mr.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Malone, it...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: would seem to...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: is that you're done?

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: I just wanted to add, Justice Breyer, that if you look at two oh six D, that calls on FERC to provide costs in your hypothetical...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: from West Virginia to the North Carolina...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Then I would say if that's your argument, the response to that argument will be this is an unforeseen case in thirty-five, the possibility of Enron supplanting Duke, and it's close enough to wholesale sales.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: If Enron supplants Duke and sells directly to a retail customer, it's going to be purchasing electricity, selling retail, over a delivery system that Enron does not own.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: May I ask you one...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: question about the words in the statute? The statute uses an and.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Not quite, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Mr. Malone, could I come back...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to Justice Ginsburg's earlier question...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: which...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: focused on section eight twenty-four B two, which says that the provisions shall not apply to any other sale of electric energy.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Including the transmission.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Including the transmission.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor, I think...

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: that is a valid reading of the statute.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: It's up to you.

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Your Honor, we have situations in New York State, for example, where we have, FERC has regulated ha- curtailments u- over transmission systems.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Very well, Mr. Malone.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: from the rules of the road.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: just chose, in its discretion, not to regulate them?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: It said that the transmissions should not be considered within the interstate transmission clause giving FERC jurisdiction over interstate transmissions, but rather, in the case of transmissions bundled with retail sales, should be considered part of the bundled retail sale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: said...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Which means that it doesn't have jurisdiction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: When you say should be...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: considered part of, you mean discretionarily should be...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: considered or must be...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: called it a permissible policy choice, and but...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And you agree with that?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: You agree it was a policy choice and not a jurisdiction...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: I think FERC made a jurisdictional mistake and I think the DC Circuit...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: It misconstrued its statute. It didn't simply withdraw its hand. It misconstrued the act.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: transmission and that it should, therefore,

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: solve the problem that it foun-

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: In your view, if FERC does have jurisdiction of this this part of the problem, must it exercise that jurisdiction or can it allow the States to regulate?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: What it must do under section two oh six, which is section eight twenty-four E, is cure the undue discrimination that it found.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, then it's...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: pretty much a Pyrrhic victory for you if you persuade us that the the s- CADC was wrong in saying it was just a policy choice, it was a jurisdictional decision.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: I'm hoping that what I had was a Pyrrhic defeat in the in the in the DC Circuit and that and that FERC, once told that it has jurisdiction, and having recognized that it has the obligation to cure undue discrimination, will exercise that jurisdiction properly.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: We we usually have cases in which the agency is alleged to go too far.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Well, actually in the Louisiana Power and Light case, which we cite a- and think is very much in point here, which arises under the same parallel provisions of the Natural Gas Act, FERC's initial position was that it I believe FERC's initial position was that it did not have jurisdiction to regulate bundled direct retail sales of o- o- of gas and that that included u- u- a a lack of jurisdiction over the transportation of that gas.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: I guess th- what would , suppose FERC says or suppose I read it as saying, since you're whipsawing them between you and New York, look, in nineteen thirty-five yours is the case where Congress would have never dreamt they had jurisdiction.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: What happened in nineteen thirty-five was that, recognizing that interconnection was coming, Congress granted FERC jurisdiction over transportation of electric energy in interstate commerce without limitation.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: It gave them, without limitation, authority over transportation of energy, but not without limitation authority over sale of energy.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Isn't that an essential part of your argument? Well, where does it where does it...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: the States have plenary power to regulate who may serve their retail customers and at what rates and at what and on what other terms and conditions.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What do you mean sa-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: take into account? I thought you're saying that that FERC has the power to determine a a a component of the retail sale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It has the power to...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: determine the the cost of transmission of electricity insofar as that and and and and that may be one of the costs that the retail utility i- incurs for which the retail utility has the right under State law to recover.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: That's right.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the transmission...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: has authority to regulate the the wholesale sale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: sale. I mean, yeah, but transmission...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: by the statute, as I read it, is separated from sale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Where are you reading f-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I'm reading from B one.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: It's eight twenty-four...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Six, sixteen. Yes.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: The provisions of this chapter shall apply to the transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: That's...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: drawing a dichotomy between transmission and sale.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: And we have stressed whereas the s- the the the nine States have tried to blur this distinction, we have stressed the distinction between FERC's jurisdiction over transmission and FERC's jurisdiction over wholesale sales.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Must be taken into account.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: is i- i- is, n- does not encroach upon the States' retail sale authority, but it is something that the States must respect in setting retail rates, so FERC's regulation of the cost of transmission.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: But FERC's regulation of the cost of of of interstate transmission at wholesale comes about not because of its authority over the transmission of el- of electric energy, but because of its authority over the sale of electric energy at wholesale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: to take that into a-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I, Yes. I don't I don't agree with that. that that...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: that is that is the statutory...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yeah. The statute...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: says, shall apply to transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well it says, doesn't it?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and, two,

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: the sale of electric energy at wholesale in interstate commerce, which you take to be a second grant of authority.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes, and the basic...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: The basic structure of our argument is that just as your opinion for the Court, Justice O'Connor, explained in Nantahala, that m- the States must take into account the wholesale cost of energy, so the States can be required to to take into account FERC's regulation of transmission...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: What difference does it make whether the transmission component is bundled or unbundled at retail sale?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: We don't think it should make any because transmissions , whether for wholesale or retail, whether bundled or unbundled, compete for use of the interstate grid.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Can you sell at...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: retail in States which are which are un- bundled?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Absolutely not.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What we want...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: then why is there then why is there a problem?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Because what we want the right to do is to use the transmission system through those States without the retail the traditional monopoly utility that owns the facilities in those States being able to hog those facilities for its own use and keep us off the road.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: let me try an example.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: trying to reach retail...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Even though we're not trying to reach retail customers, we may be trying to sell e- energy to the utility in Minneso- in in in in in Wisconsin.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The traditional...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: then that wholesale sale can be regulated.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: The wholesale sale can be regulated, but the traditional utility in Minnesota, which owns the facilities and mostly makes bundled retail sales to its customers in Minnesota, can say, our bundled retail sales are not subject to FERC's OATT.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: very surprised that FERC is saying that they have no jurisdiction over that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: of this statute, isn't it?

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: And if B puts something up on those lines that blocks the transmission, I'm very surprised that FERC would say they don't have jurisdiction to stop...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, we were a...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: little surprised too.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: But that's what this case is about in your view.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: From our s- from our standpoint, that's what our side of this case is about.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: They said...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: that would be an interference with the transmission, not with the sale.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: authority over that because they have...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: have authority over...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: transmission,

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: both interstate and intrastate.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: All that I'm asking the Court to say is that FERC has the ability to a-, the jurisdiction to apply its general open access transmission tariff not only to our sales a- f- at wholesale and to s- but but also to the bundled retail sales that compete with us for use of the o- of the transmission grids.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Well, if if the , FERC can regulate your transmissions and you go to FERC and say, you know, our transmissions are being blocked, why can't they say, we will exercise jurisdiction over y- y- your transmission and we will unblock you?

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Justice Kennedy, the the answer to that i- is, first of all, that it's it's not just blockage.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: transmission.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: thought you wanted the particular reg there's a particular reg that New York has been talking about, and I thought you wanted that applied to the , a- bundled sales as well.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: e- Particular regulation.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Yeah.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: And and and and that seems to me a different issue than somebody climbing up a pole, for example, and cutting the wire so that you can't get your electricity between State A and State...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: C.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: the ideal example is the curtailment example.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: customers, and that's...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Government to determine which which will be the emergency users and the preferred users in each State and locality.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: We want the Federal Government...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: to regulate the transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce, and that has become a more complicated problem than it was in nineten thirty-five.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: There's always been a product.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It's it's it's a lesser percentage now than it used to...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: on average it's now about it's now about ten percent.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: I don't know about you, but when I look at my utility rate, it's a very small amount for transmission and a substantially larger amount for the energy.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: It is.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: I don't even see how this works.

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: Well, in order to have the the the electricity flows...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: the the the power flows generally over the system, but...

turns.name: LOUIS R. COHEN
turns.text: but in order to have the right to turn on, to ramp up your generator to to produce some additional electricity which your customer a- a- a- at a time when your customer is going to take electricity off the system at some other point, y- you need to have a contractual arrangement or other right to do that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Justice.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Kneedler, we'll hear from you.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Mr. Kneedler, it it if I understand it correctly, it is i- the position of the agency hinges on the proposition that the the power conferred by by section eight twenty-four to regulate the transmission of electrical energy includes includes the power to regulate the sale of the transmission of elec- electrical energy.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Isn't that what...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: the sale of the transmission,

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: The sale of the transmission.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: does not in- what it does not include is the retail sale.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Now, if that were true, it seems to me that eight twenty-four B one, instead of reading the way it does, should read this way, The provisions of this subchapter shall apply to the transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce which would include the sale of that transmission, right? And to the sale of the commodity portion of electrical energy at wholesale in interstate commerce.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Well th-

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And but...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: so, it should have gone on to say, and to the sale of the of the commodity at wholesale in interstate commerce.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But th- but that is what it says I believe because...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: electric energy is the commodity.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: FERC, that's been...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I'm sorry.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: B one?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: B one, yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And and and you think...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: that that sale of electric energy at wholesale in in the first portion of it, in in one, only referred to the sale of regulating the sale of the commodity at wholesale.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Well, yes, but it, but for example, in setting a wholesale cost or excuse me a wholesale rate, FERC could take into account the costs of transmission...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Because...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: wholesale rate, just as the State could.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Because of its...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: authority over the transmission of energy.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Either one.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: transmission service.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: very unrealistic reading of what Congress thought it was doing when it wrote this thing, that that when it when it spoke of the sale of electric energy at wholesale in interstate commerce, it had in mind only only the sale of the the the commodity and not the sale of, you know, the transmission and the entire cost.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yes, absolute-.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: part of it.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: can't say that because if if you say that that that that that that the reason you have that authority is because of the phrase, the sale of electric energy at wholesale, you would also have to say that in the very next phrase, the sale of elect- electric energy any other sale of electric energy, sale at retail, would also include the transmission component.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Okay, and if I can...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to exclude the transmission component from both of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: them it seems to me.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: if if I could answer that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I don't think...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: that's unquestionable at all.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: But well, it's it's an independent grant of authority.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Well, suppose you have have a condition, as des- described by u- uh counsel for Enron, and they say, we just can't transmit our gas through this State.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yeah, we, absolutely.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: FERC's...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: discretionary...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: yeah, Well, there are...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: two different things, and I and I think it was maybe you who put the question.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: been handled so, but but that...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: different things.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Right. And and as...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: that you have that?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yea- w-, We n-- don't just concede, we affirmatively assert that we do have authority.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: States...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: jurisdiction case.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Not in that respect, no, because I I think that all that,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: with respect to this order...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: objection to our saying and I don't know quite how we'd say it, that the FCC has jurisdiction over transmissions of bundled sales at retail.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Well, l- let me let me just modify that with in in one respect.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: service...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: just a two-step pro- say, you s- say, first, unbundle, and now that you've unbundled,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: we're going to regulate it. Th- that's...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Exa- and and the court of ap- the, and the, what the cou- the court of appeals analysis in this case essentially looked at the at the at the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: transaction...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: we can get back to my quest-.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And that...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: jurisdiction is is thought of in in in constitutional terms, I, th- the answer is pretty clear.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I believe FERC does. I...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I believe FERC does.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: I mean, that's essentially what FERC...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: ultimately did in the natural gas area.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: But aren't aren't you...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: but l- let me be clear.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the traditional...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What FERC has...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: puzzled because I th- I mu- I must have misread the papers.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: wants it to take. Yes.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: That that may well have been an an an implicit understanding of FERC, but if you look at the order, it actually never says that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: permissible...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: position of the Government is, in respect to Enron's case, not New York's, Enron is right.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: what happened here. That, is that...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: is that your position or not?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Th-, I I I cannot speak for FERC on that question because FERC did not address that in this order.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: But I want to know what I I have an argument in some briefs made by Enron , and perhaps I misread them and I'll go back to them.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Well, i- if there are really two points about FERC's power here, or a- authority here and that that that I would would like to separate.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: any impediments to use...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: how do I answer this question from your question presented? Number two, in oh oh eight oh nine, the question presented is whether the court of appeals properly deferred to the commission's determination that it lacks jurisdiction over retail transmission service that is sold together with electric energy in a single bundled transaction, et cetera.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: properly defer?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: did properly defer because the predicate is that there is a single transaction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: of the one bundled transaction.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: that i- a- are you drawing this line? You're saying that FERC has the authority to order unbundling.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and under the...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: saying that you see nothing in the act that would prevent FERC from doing just what Mr. Cohen wants it to do so long as it takes two steps, so long as it orders unbundling first.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I- i- i- Assuming that FERC has this authority as they stated, they didn't disclaim it, but FERC in this order...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: did not did not stress that. I'm a I'm a I'm a I see...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: nothing in in the act in the parallel provisions of the Natural Gas Act...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Well, a- a- again, r- referring to the question presented that you drafted, question two, the question presented is whether the court of appeals properly referred to the commission's determination that it lacks jurisdiction over retail transmission.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: i- But the rest of the question is, when it is sold together with electric energy in a single bundled transaction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: transaction that exists by...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: yes, it lacks jurisdiction.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yes, yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: In that circumstance. Therefore,

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: when Mr. Cohen put the example that he did of a grid, and the grid covers several States, and there are many sales for wholesales between sales for resale between A and C and D and F, but there's one in the middle, C, where it in fact is all bundled.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: And and if in fact they are scheduling deliveries, the State commission, in that C so that the wrong people are getting the energy and so that the wholesales the sales for resale in A, E, and F are all mucked up, the answer is, of course, the commission has jurisdiction...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes. Yes. Absolutely.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Even even even under Order eight eighty-eight, and a- what Order eight eighty-eight does is decline to extend the plenary rate jurisdi- the plenary jurisdiction of Order eight eighty-eight itself over the transmission component of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: bundled sales.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: thing it doesn't have jurisdiction over, in your opinion, is to make this kind of nondiscriminatory order applicable in our middle State C where the transactions are all run from generating to distribution to transmission, everything is run by one company, P G and E.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Right. The Right. As long as they...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: as long as they are bun- as long as the State hasn't unbundled it, the utility has not unbundled it, and FERC has not exercised whatever authority it would have...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: to unbundle it...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Even though they are in the grid.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: See, I didn't know that...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: let me let me let me make let make clear.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: So and your reason,

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: as I interpret it, in a sentence is if we go back to nineteen thirty-five, even if the New York situation was something they never would have dreamt of, the Enron situation is something that they certainly did dream of.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: They...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: covering that.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: I right? Don't say I'm right if I'm not.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Where? Where where where does the statute you you say the statute leaves the retail rates.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: are you referring to?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: in the clause in two oh one B that you were referring to, shall not apply to any other sale of electric energy.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Yeah.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: That is leaving to the States the the authority to set the rates for the retail sale of electric energy, which is essentially what was left to them under the Pennsylvania Gas case as a as a matter of the Interstate Commerce Clause.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Mr. Kneedler, would you please clarify what FERC has no jurisdiction that is, no authority Congress gave them no authority to do what versus what they can do or decline to do as a matter of policy? As I read Judge Sentelle's portion of the per curiam opinion, those two were put together, and they're two quite different things.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I I think what is no power is where the the transaction remains bundled, FERC may characterize that in the same way that it has always been treated, as as a single retail rate, and that FERC cannot require, as long as the transaction remains bundled, that the that that a separate cost component for retail transmission be stated.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: You mean ma-? You say, may characterize it.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Y- Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: point.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: if you're going to say it has no power, you have to say must...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: characterize it, not may characterize it.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I I don't have to say it depends what you mean by must because the cou- the the co- the court of appeals did not say that was the only way to read the statute.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: have as a statue of appeal...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: you- your your position, and it's it's it's a very odd answer to a question to say that we may say we may have no jurisdiction, I've, we have no jurisdiction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: jurisdiction.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: point.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: If you have the power to characterize it one way or the other, it seems to me you have the power to assert jurisdiction.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: jurisdiction.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: matter, FERC has has concluded has construed the statute not to authorize it to under Chevron, the the the agency...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: could go either way.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: done differently, it could move in to regulate it all.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: But but FERC has con- now construed the statute, in a statutorily permissible way, that does not give it jurisdiction over the transmission component of bundled retail sales.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: May I ask this question?

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: the power on that reading unless Congress gave it to them.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Right,

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: as long as the transaction remains bundled.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: which...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: you this question? If we assume we assume that the commission has said that they do not have jurisdiction when the transaction remains bundled, because it wasn't voluntarily unbundled or compelled to be unbundled by State or Federal authority.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: jurisdiction?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: think I think FERC would have great authority in deciding how to identify discrimination and how to deal with it.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It's all at the sufferance of...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: FERC, however.

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: Well, I I the- there may well be limitations on that because, among other things, the the the act preserves to the States the authority over local distribution systems and over retail rates.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: power a- allocation...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: has has determined, after a hearing, that there is discrimination going on in the allocation of transmission services, must it then order a remedy?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I think if it finds discrimination, it has to order appropriate relief.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: And are there still some intrastate electric producers that also transmit their energy totally within the State and FERC has no jurisdiction over...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that at all?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: if they if you- if a util- if such utility is connected to the grid, it cannot transmit...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But probably...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: there are some that are not connected...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Very, I'm informed...

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: by FERC that there are virtually none anymore, and that that that while the the act does preserve intrastate transmission to the to the States, there's virtually none that isn't connected to the grid.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: remain...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: regulates a bundled retail sale, it is, in, all, for all practical purposes, in part regulating transmission?

turns.name: EDWIN S. KNEEDLER
turns.text: I would say it is not regulating transmission, and and I and I think that that is what the Court said in in Pennsylvania in Pennsylvania Gas.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Kneedler.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: LAWRENCE G. MALONE
turns.text: Your Honor, Justice Scalia, in response to your question as to whether FERC has given the authority to the State to decide prioritization of curtailment, the answer is no.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Thank you very much.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Malone.
